typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Finalization enforcement remains procedural-only: changes are docs + script, but validator runtime finalization still does not enforce required-flow-report persistence before success.
verify-flow-report-finalization defaults to core-engine-specific report names (settings-ui/launch-contracts), so default contract is not milestone-generic and can misclassify valid runs.

Backfilled traceability cites implementation commit 61153f... that updates epsilon-tolerance assertions, not compile-drift parameter wiring (cacheReadTokens/cacheCreationTokens/requestCount), leaving scope linkage unresolved.
Handoff commitId remains malformed/non-resolvable, so the primary machine-auditable commit linkage requirement is still not met despite improved textual evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce handoff metadata gates automatically: reject/flag `followedProcedure: true` without explicit context-read evidence and reject non-resolvable commitId values before handoff acceptance.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple misc-infra-followups rerun reviews still found procedural compliance claims with missing evidence, and one fix handoff used malformed commitId while marked as procedure-following.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Route misc-infra/script/traceability features to an infra-appropriate worker skill instead of switcher-integration-worker to reduce repeated procedure mismatch and noisy compliance reporting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three of four rerun feature reviews observed switcher-integration-worker procedure misfit for infra/traceability work in this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add immediate library documentation updates for validator-runtime file ownership.
Per-feature repeats that switcher-integration-worker is a poor fit.
